source_note: Use with layoff-defense/references/legal-map.md. Verify current law and local rules before final advice.

separation_agreement.description: Mutual termination, settlement, departure, or compensation agreement.

resignation_form.description: Employee resignation letter, OA resignation form, voluntary resignation template, or personal-reason resignation document.

non_compete_agreement.description: Post-employment or in-term non-compete agreement, clause, notice, or release.

confidentiality_agreement.description: Confidentiality, trade secret, IP, customer information, or data protection agreement.

service_period_agreement.description: Training, special treatment, service-period, or training-cost repayment agreement.

termination_notice_or_certificate.description: Termination notice, termination certificate, departure confirmation, or receipt.

all_claims_waiver.label: All claims settled / no further disputes / rights waiver
all_claims_waiver.worker_side_risk: May release unpaid wages, overtime, annual leave, bonus, social insurance-related claims, double wage, unlawful termination, or future discovered claims.
all_claims_waiver.recommended_edit: Carve out unpaid statutory items, social insurance/housing fund, tax, non-compete compensation, undiscovered claims, and claims caused by employer breach or non-payment.
all_claims_waiver.evidence_impact: Preserve all drafts and communications showing whether the employer proposed the release and whether pressure, misunderstanding, or unfairness existed.

resignation_characterization.label: Voluntary resignation / personal reasons / employee-initiated departure
resignation_characterization.worker_side_risk: Can weaken economic compensation and unlawful termination claims if it contradicts forced resignation or employer breach facts.
resignation_characterization.recommended_edit: Use factual wording: employer proposed termination, parties negotiate termination, or worker terminates because of specific employer statutory breach when evidence supports it.
resignation_characterization.evidence_impact: Preserve employer pressure, unpaid wage/social insurance records, lockout, transfer, salary cut, and objection records.

payment_amount_and_date.label: Compensation amount, payment deadline, tax, and default liability
payment_amount_and_date.worker_side_risk: Unclear amount, tax basis, payment date, or default clause can make enforcement harder and reduce actual recovery.
payment_amount_and_date.recommended_edit: State itemized gross amount, payment date, bank account, tax treatment, social insurance month, overdue liability, and no offset unless agreed in writing.
payment_amount_and_date.evidence_impact: Preserve wage basis, settlement sheet, payment schedule, bank records, and 12-month wage proof.

handover_condition.label: Handover, return of property, account deletion, and conditional payment
handover_condition.worker_side_risk: Overbroad handover or deletion duties can destroy evidence or let employer delay payment.
handover_condition.recommended_edit: Define handover list, deadline, receipt, lawful evidence preservation, and payment not unreasonably withheld for disputed handover.
handover_condition.evidence_impact: Preserve handover checklist, receipt, account lockout timing, and lawful copies of personal employment records.

termination_certificate.label: Termination certificate and departure reason wording
termination_certificate.worker_side_risk: Incorrect reason can affect unemployment benefits, future employment explanation, and dispute characterization.
termination_certificate.recommended_edit: State neutral lawful wording, termination date, position, service years, and avoid untrue voluntary resignation or misconduct labels.
termination_certificate.evidence_impact: Preserve certificate drafts and employer refusal or delay records.

non_compete.label: Non-compete scope, period, compensation, release, and breach exposure
non_compete.worker_side_risk: Overbroad scope, no compensation, wrong personnel scope, excessive geography or term, and high liquidated damages can block new work or create major liability.
non_compete.recommended_edit: Limit personnel basis, protected secrets, competitors, region, duration, compensation amount/date, release mechanism, and breach calculation.
non_compete.evidence_impact: Preserve role, access to secrets/IP, compensation payments, employer release notice, and intended new role facts.

confidentiality_and_penalty.label: Confidentiality, trade secrets, IP, customer data, and penalty clauses
confidentiality_and_penalty.worker_side_risk: Confidentiality can be used as disguised non-compete or penalty clause beyond statutory liquidated damages limits.
confidentiality_and_penalty.recommended_edit: Define confidential information narrowly, exclude public/known/worker-owned information, remove disguised non-compete and unsupported penalty language.
confidentiality_and_penalty.evidence_impact: Preserve job scope and actual access to confidential matters; do not copy company secrets as evidence.

liquidated_damages.label: Worker-paid liquidated damages, penalty, clawback, or indemnity
liquidated_damages.worker_side_risk: Worker-paid penalties are generally limited to statutory service-period and non-compete contexts; broad indemnity may be invalid or excessive.
liquidated_damages.recommended_edit: Delete non-statutory penalties; cap training repayment to remaining allocable training cost; tie non-compete damages to effective agreement and actual breach.
liquidated_damages.evidence_impact: Preserve training invoices, special treatment proof, non-compete compensation proof, and employer loss calculation.

service_period.label: Service period, training cost repayment, and special treatment
service_period.worker_side_risk: Employer may claim repayment or damages after early resignation; enforceability depends on special training/treatment, actual cost, fault, and remaining period.
service_period.recommended_edit: Require itemized training cost, actual invoices, amortization schedule, service-period end date, and statutory解除 carve-outs.
service_period.evidence_impact: Preserve training content, invoice, attendance, certificate, and whether it was ordinary onboarding or special technical training.

review_outputs.must_fix_before_signing: Clauses with critical/high risk or missing essential terms.
review_outputs.negotiate: Clauses that should be narrowed, clarified, or priced.
review_outputs.verify_amounts: Payment, tax, wage-base, cap, non-compete compensation, or service-period cost checks.
review_outputs.lawyer_check: Non-compete, stock options, executive status, foreign worker, high-value settlement, group layoff, or broad release.

global_safety_rules: Do not draft fake resignation reasons, fake payment receipts, or backdated agreements.
Do not advise signing broad all-claims waivers before amounts, payment date, and carve-outs are clear.
Do not tell the worker to copy company secrets or customer data as evidence.
If already signed, analyze validity, revocation, performance, and non-payment instead of pretending the document is unsigned.
